title: WIN-053: Surface-aperture re-interpretation defender pivot foreclosed (closes ISS-1243)
target: data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence — single find_replace patch inserting a new ~270-word paragraph between the closing-of-rescues sentence and the 'second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form' paragraph. The patch addresses a defender pivot distinct from the existing narrow-channel-return rescue: that the south coefficient C = 64,852 nT describes not a sub-terranean return density but a second surface aperture (above the Sump, where the toroidal flow re-enters at the Antarctic barrier), with A and C as field magnitudes at two symmetric surface apertures. Two structural rebuttals close the rescue: (i) symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly while the fitted values differ by 4% and that 4% tracks WMM/IGRF pole asymmetry — so the coefficients are curve-fit to observation or implicitly borrowing geodynamo asymmetry; (ii) both coefficients carry positive signs (both apertures as flux sources), so the directional sign contradiction documented earlier in the same paragraph cluster persists unchanged.
curmudgeon_review: ISS-1243 inherits the curmudgeon recommendation embedded in the issue description (WIN-053.c5 H3). Curmudgeon's recommended preemption text is faithful in substance and used directly as the rebuttal skeleton; this EXP expands the curmudgeon-recommended ~75-word preemption into a ~270-word paragraph that (a) acknowledges the kernel of truth explicitly (a closed two-aperture circuit does demand similar fields), (b) breaks the rebuttal into two enumerated grounds for clarity, and (c) cross-cites WMM2025 and IGRF-13 with the same NCEI URLs used elsewhere in WIN-053.detail_evidence for citation consistency.
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T11:40Z; deep-attack class because the work adds a previously-absent defender pivot rebuttal and is not pure verification)
class_hint_honored: deep-attack (HONORED — issue class_hint is 'deep-attack' and tracker review_class is 'deep-attack'; no override)
review_class_rationale: ISS-1243 introduces a defender pivot distinct from the existing narrow-channel-return rescue already addressed in WIN-053.detail_evidence. The new pivot requires constructing fresh adversarial argumentation and cross-citing observational data (WMM2025/IGRF-13 asymmetry) to close it. This is argument-strengthening singleton work, comparable in shape to EXP-349 (WIN-014 off-path defender pivot foreclosure). Verification-class batching is inappropriate — curmudgeon needs to re-attack the new paragraph for missed defender escapes (e.g., 'maybe the 4% asymmetry is intrinsic to dome geometry'), which is a singleton-review task.
summary_for_decider:
EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243 closes ISS-1243 (WIN-053.c5 H3, moderate, orphan-picked-up by analyst-baby and escalated to Opus 2026-05-14) with a single targeted find_replace patch on data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence. The patch inserts a new ~270-word paragraph between the existing close of the narrow-channel-return rescue ('not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.') and the 'second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form' paragraph. The new paragraph addresses a DIFFERENT defender pivot: the surface-aperture re-interpretation (C describes a south surface aperture, not a sub-terranean return density). Kernel-of-truth acknowledgment: a closed two-aperture circuit DOES demand similar field magnitudes at both ends, and the dome's fitted A and C are similar (~62,000–65,000 nT). The paragraph acknowledges this directly before showing why the rescue still fails. Two independent structural rebuttals close the pivot: (i) Symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly, while the fitted values differ by ~4% and that 4% tracks the observed WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole asymmetry — so the coefficients are either curve-fit to observation or implicitly borrowing the asymmetric geodynamo physics the dome was built to leave behind. (ii) Both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources. The directional sign contradiction documented earlier in the same paragraph cluster (positive C at the 'sink' end) is unchanged by the re-interpretation. Anchor uniqueness verified: 'not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.

A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.' appears exactly once in data/wins.json (count = 1, verified via regex match). No new tests required (DATA-blob anti-pattern not triggered; no chart arrays touched; word counts inside detail_evidence have no pinned floor/ceiling). Recommended action: PATCH; close ISS-1243 as fixed-pending-verification on the next WIN-053 curmudgeon cycle.

decision.rationale: Option A (single find_replace patch inserting the new defender-pivot rebuttal paragraph between the closed narrow-channel rescue and the functional-form paragraph) is correct because: (1) the surface-aperture re-interpretation IS a live and distinct defender pivot — the existing detail_evidence rebuts narrow-channel-return and Maxwellian-vs-aetheric rescues, but does not address the surface-aperture re-interpretation; (2) the curmudgeon's own recommended preemption text in ISS-1243 explicitly calls out the A=C-by-construction rebuttal and the WMM-asymmetry rebuttal — this patch implements those rebuttals with the curmudgeon's wording as skeleton; (3) inserting the paragraph between the two existing structural-contradiction paragraphs preserves the existing paragraph flow and does not disturb the cross-references to §6.1, §6.4, WIN-012, INTERMAGNET, CHAOS-7, or the South Atlantic Anomaly citation; (4) the kernel-of-truth standard is honored — the new paragraph explicitly acknowledges 'a closed two-aperture circuit demands similar field magnitudes at both ends' before showing why the rescue fails.
decision.why_not_option_b: Option B (rewrite the existing 'narrow channel' rescue paragraph to also cover surface-aperture) was rejected because: (i) the surface-aperture pivot is structurally distinct — it does not invoke a narrow sub-terranean channel and so does not share the 'mantle-and-crust upward continuation' rebuttal that closes the narrow-channel rescue; (ii) merging both rescues into a single paragraph would force the prose to fork on every sentence (one rebuttal for narrow-channel, another for surface-aperture), losing readability; (iii) the existing narrow-channel paragraph is well-formed and already cited (INTERMAGNET, CHAOS-7); rewriting it risks introducing citation breakage; (iv) a fresh paragraph between the two existing paragraphs is the minimally-disruptive addition.
decision.why_not_option_c: Option C (decline to patch on the grounds that the existing detail_evidence already establishes the dome contradicts ∇·B = 0 regardless of which surface vs sub-terranean interpretation one picks) was rejected because: (i) the curmudgeon's H3 critique is substantive, not cosmetic — the surface-aperture pivot IS a live rescue route until structurally closed in the prose; (ii) leaving the pivot unaddressed means a hostile reader can cite it as 'they didn't think of this rescue'; (iii) the patch is ~270 words — modest addition, not a major rewrite; (iv) the kernel-of-truth standard requires acknowledging that the two-symmetric-aperture geometry IS a natural reading of the dome's own claimed toroidal architecture, then showing why the dome's specific fitted values are incompatible with that reading.

patches.target_path: WIN-053.detail_evidence — new paragraph inserted between the close of the narrow-channel rescue paragraph (ends '...not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.') and the start of the functional-form-contradiction paragraph (begins 'A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.')

patches.find:
not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.

A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.
patches.replace:
not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.

A fourth charitable reading drops the sub-terranean framing entirely: perhaps C = 64,852 nT describes a second surface aperture — the point above the Sump where the toroidal flow re-enters the Earth at the Antarctic barrier — making A and C the field magnitudes at two symmetric surface apertures rather than a surface value paired with a sub-terranean return density. The kernel is real: a closed two-aperture circuit demands similar field magnitudes at both ends, and the dome's fitted A and C are similar (~62,000–65,000 nT). But this rescue fails on two independent grounds. (i) Symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly (two apertures of equal area must produce equal surface fields: AN × BN = AS × BS with AN = AS forces BN = BS). The dome's fitted values are not equal — they differ by ~4% (62,376 nT vs 64,852 nT) — and that 4% asymmetry tracks the observed WMM2025 / IGRF-13 pole-strength asymmetry to within fitting noise. So either the coefficients are tuned to globe observations (a curve fit, not a derivation from any symmetric dome geometry) or the dome is implicitly borrowing the asymmetric-geodynamo physics it was built to leave behind. (ii) Both coefficients carry positive signs in B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources rather than a source-and-sink pair. In any closed magnetic circuit one aperture must be a sink — the directional sign contradiction documented above is unchanged by re-interpreting C as a second surface aperture. Re-interpreting the south coefficient relocates the contradictions; it does not eliminate them.

A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.
patches.rationale: Closes ISS-1243 (WIN-053.c5 H3) by inserting a new ~270-word paragraph addressing the surface-aperture re-interpretation defender pivot — a rescue route distinct from the existing narrow-channel-return and Maxwellian-vs-aetheric rescues already foreclosed in detail_evidence. Two structural rebuttals: (i) symmetric flux conservation requires A = C exactly while the fitted values differ by 4% and that 4% tracks WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole asymmetry, so the coefficients are curve-fit to observation or implicitly borrowing geodynamo asymmetry; (ii) both coefficients carry positive signs (treating both apertures as flux sources), so the directional sign contradiction documented earlier persists. The kernel-of-truth (a closed two-aperture circuit demands similar fields) is acknowledged head-on. WMM2025 and IGRF-13 are cited with the same NCEI URLs used elsewhere in WIN-053.detail_evidence for citation consistency. No new external citations required.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: C = 64,852 nT describes not a sub-terranean return-channel field but a second SURFACE aperture — the point above the Sump where the toroidal flow re-enters at the Antarctic barrier — making A and C the field magnitudes at two symmetric surface apertures, which is what a closed two-aperture flux circuit demands.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Directly closed by the patch via two independent grounds. (i) Symmetric flux conservation requires A = C by construction (two apertures of equal area must produce equal surface fields). The dome's fitted values differ by ~4% (62,376 vs 64,852 nT), and that 4% asymmetry tracks the observed WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole-strength asymmetry to within fitting noise. So either the coefficients are tuned to globe observations (a curve fit) or the dome is implicitly borrowing the asymmetric-geodynamo physics the dome was built to leave behind. (ii) Both coefficients carry positive signs, so the dome treats both apertures as flux SOURCES rather than a source-and-sink pair. The directional sign contradiction documented earlier in the same paragraph cluster persists unchanged.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The 4% asymmetry between A and C could be intrinsic to the dome's geometry — perhaps the Axis Mundi aperture and the Antarctic re-entry aperture have slightly different physical areas, so A ≠ C is expected.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged implicitly via 'tracks the observed WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole-strength asymmetry to within fitting noise'. The dome would need to publish an INDEPENDENT derivation of the aperture-area ratio that produces the specific 4% asymmetry observed (not a post-hoc fit). No such derivation exists on the dome site or in monitor.py; the coefficients A and C are presented as fitted constants with no link to any dome-geometric parameter. The 'intrinsic geometry' rescue requires the dome to specify A_N/A_S as an independent parameter and derive the 4% asymmetry from it, which the dome does not do.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The directional sign contradiction is a misreading — perhaps the dome's B(r) is a magnitude expression and the sign convention is implicitly handled by the toroidal flow direction.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Even if one grants this, the rescue fails because the dome explicitly fits two POSITIVE coefficients to OBSERVED surface field magnitudes — both observed surface fields point OUT of the Earth at the geographic poles (north pole observed field points down-and-out, south pole observed field points up-and-out, both with outward-radial components). A toroidal source-sink architecture would require one of those observed fields to point INWARD at the surface, which contradicts the observed WMM/IGRF field-direction data. The 'implicit sign convention' rescue cannot reconcile the directional data with a closed two-aperture toroidal circuit.

kernel_of_truth.acknowledged: ISS-1243's defender is correct on the structural setup: a closed two-aperture flux circuit DOES demand similar field magnitudes at both apertures, and the dome's fitted A = 62,376 nT and C = 64,852 nT ARE similar (~62,000–65,000 nT). Read narrowly, the dome's two-coefficient surface fit superficially matches what a symmetric closed circuit would produce. The author of the H3 critique correctly identifies that our existing detail_evidence does not explicitly address this surface-aperture interpretation — it rebuts the narrow-channel-return rescue (where C is sub-terranean) and the Maxwellian-vs-aetheric rescue (where ∇·B = 0 is waived), but leaves the surface-aperture re-interpretation as a live rescue route.
kernel_of_truth.why_does_not_save_dome: Acknowledging the surface-aperture interpretation does not rescue the dome because the structural argument operates one level above the surface/sub-terranean choice: (i) a symmetric closed circuit demands A = C exactly, but the fitted values differ by 4% and that 4% tracks the observed WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole-strength asymmetry (which exists because Earth's actual geodynamo is asymmetric, not because the dome's symmetric toroidal architecture predicts it); (ii) both coefficients carry positive signs in the fitted B(r), so the dome treats both apertures as flux sources, which contradicts the source-sink topology of any closed circuit regardless of where the sink is physically located. The kernel-of-truth (two-aperture similarity) is real but does not load-bear on the verdict; the verdict load-bears on the specific fitted asymmetry tracking observation and on the sign convention treating both poles as sources.

next_steps_for_decider: Apply single find_replace patch against data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence per EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243-A-WIN053-surface-aperture-rescue. No code changes; no test changes; no JS rebuild required.
